artifacts.description: Resolved benchmark request embedded in generated runners.
Build metadata embedded beside the benchmark spec.
Generated Android app and instrumentation packages.
Normalized profile manifest, plus a latest convenience copy.
Profile summary, plus a latest convenience copy.
Processed native stack samples when capture succeeds.
Symbolized native profiler report when available.
Full and benchmark-focused flamegraph SVGs when capture succeeds.
Standalone profile viewer when capture succeeds.

capabilities.summary: Build generated Android benchmark artifacts.
Build generated iOS benchmark artifacts.
Execute Android benchmarks through BrowserStack App Automate when devices and credentials are provided.
Execute iOS benchmarks through BrowserStack XCUITest when devices, credentials, and packages are provided.
Build and enumerate mobile artifacts without BrowserStack credentials or write-capable permissions.
Verify a prebuilt manifest before credentialed BrowserStack upload, execution, and collection.
Keep untrusted report rendering separate from credentialed benchmark execution.
Run an optional normalized caller preparation hook only in secretless, read-only prepare jobs.
Select platform-specific function lists and structured multi-device inputs in the reusable workflow.
Fail closed unless every requested function and device has exactly one result shard.
`--local-only` writes preflight/spec outputs but skips the mobile build and does not execute the requested benchmark in v0.1.45.
Ordinary `mobench run` does not execute on an attached phone in v0.1.45.
Ordinary `mobench run` has no Android emulator execution provider in v0.1.45.
Ordinary `mobench run` has no iOS simulator execution provider in v0.1.45.
With no BrowserStack devices, `mobench run` builds artifacts and skips upload/execution.
Attempt local Android simpleperf capture, symbolization, and flamegraph generation when required tools and a device are available.
Attempt local iOS simulator-host sample capture and flamegraph generation when required tools are available.
The manifest/trace contract exists, but local rust-tracing capture is not implemented.
BrowserStack native Android stack capture is not implemented.
BrowserStack native iOS Instruments capture is not implemented.
BrowserStack rust-tracing capture is not implemented.
